name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery spots on leaves and stems
applicationMethod: Spray every 7 days on affected areas
precautions: Apply in early morning; avoid during flowering

name: Aphid Infestation
symptoms: Curled yellow leaves, sticky honeydew residue on foliage
applicationMethod: Spray every 4 days, covering undersides of leaves

name: Late Blight
symptoms: Dark water-soaked lesions on stems and leaves, white mold in humid conditions
applicationMethod: Spray weekly; remove and destroy infected parts

name: Rust
symptoms: Orange or brown pustules on leaf undersides, yellowing foliage
applicationMethod: Apply every 10 days until symptoms subside
organicAlternative: Baking soda solution (1 tbsp per gallon)

name: Root Rot
symptoms: Wilting despite watering, yellowing leaves, mushy brown roots

name: Leaf Spot
symptoms: Circular brown or black spots on leaves, sometimes with yellow halos
organicAlternative: Remove affected leaves; spray with diluted milk

name: Spider Mite Damage
symptoms: Fine webbing on leaves, stippled yellow/bronze foliage

name: Bacterial Wilt
symptoms: Sudden wilting of entire plant, brown vascular tissue when stem is cut
applicationMethod: Soil drench at first sign of wilt
organicAlternative: Crop rotation; remove infected plants immediately
precautions: No cure once established – prevention is key
